title: synostemon elachophyllus subsp. elachophyllus
intro: Synostemon elachophyllus subsp. elachophyllus is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the desert lantern tree. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues, due to its flavonoids and terpenoids content. In herbal preparations, Synostemon elachophyllus subsp. elachophyllus is often used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
